traders.name: distinct-baguette
traders.description: The #1 15-minute crypto trader on Polymarket. Buys dips, sells peaks, 142s median hold.
traders.longDescription: distinct-baguette is the highest-performing 15-minute crypto prediction market bot on Polymarket. It trades BTC, ETH, SOL, and XRP on 15-minute binary outcome windows, consistently buying at dips and selling at peaks with a median hold time of just 142 seconds. Its strategy appears to combine rapid mean-reversion entries with tight risk management, exiting positions well before the window settles. The bot has demonstrated strong alpha generation across volatile and low-volatility market conditions alike.

traders.name: abrak25
traders.description: High-frequency 5-minute crypto prediction trader. $13K+ daily P&L since Feb 2026.
traders.longDescription: abrak25 is a high-frequency automated trader specializing in Polymarket's 5-minute crypto prediction markets. Since becoming active in February 2026, it has generated over $13,000 in daily P&L through rapid-fire entries across BTC, ETH, SOL, and XRP markets. The bot places dozens of trades per window, using what appears to be a scalping strategy that captures small price movements within the 5-minute binary outcome markets. Its high trade volume and consistent profitability mark it as one of the most active bots in the Polymarket ecosystem.

traders.name: 0x8dxd
traders.description: Hourly crypto market trader. Trades BTC, ETH, SOL, XRP on 1-hour prediction windows.
traders.longDescription: 0x8dxd operates on Polymarket's 1-hour crypto prediction markets, trading BTC, ETH, SOL, and XRP binary outcomes. Unlike the faster 5-minute and 15-minute bots, this trader takes a more patient approach — building positions over the course of the hour-long window and managing inventory across multiple coins. The longer time horizon allows for larger position sizes and more deliberate entry timing, making its trade patterns particularly useful for studying hourly prediction market dynamics.

traders.name: vague-sourdough
traders.longDescription: vague-sourdough is an automated 5-minute crypto prediction market trader on Polymarket. It competes in the same rapid-fire 5-minute binary outcome markets as other short-duration bots, trading across BTC, ETH, SOL, and XRP. Its trading patterns provide insight into how different bots approach the same 5-minute windows with varying strategies and risk tolerances.

traders.name: vidarx
traders.longDescription: vidarx is an automated trading bot operating on Polymarket's 5-minute crypto prediction markets. It actively trades BTC, ETH, SOL, and XRP binary outcomes, competing head-to-head with other 5-minute bots in the arena. Studying its entries and exits alongside other bots in the same market windows reveals how different algorithmic approaches perform under identical market conditions.

traders.name: Qualitative
traders.description: Published profitable bot strategy, then got targeted and lost ~$5K in 18 hours.
traders.longDescription: Qualitative made headlines in the Polymarket bot community by publishing a detailed article analyzing profitable bot strategies (read the X thread) — only to be immediately targeted by competing traders who exploited the revealed tactics. Within 18 hours of the article going live, the bot lost approximately $5,000 as adversarial bots adapted their strategies to counter the published approach. This cautionary tale demonstrates the adversarial nature of automated prediction market trading, where transparency can quickly turn alpha into a liability. The incident sparked debate about information asymmetry and whether sharing trading insights accelerates market efficiency or simply hands competitors a roadmap to your downfall.
